c74object.name: fluid.onsetfeature~
c74object.description: Calculate the spectral difference feature used by OnsetSlice.

discussion.p: Calculates the feature used by fluid.onsetslice~ for slicing in realtime.
The metric for calculating difference can be chosen from a curated selection, lending the algorithm toward slicing a broad range of musical materials.
Read more about fluid.onsetfeature~ on the learn platform.
fluid.onsetfeature~ is part of the Fluid Decomposition Toolkit of the FluCoMa project. For more explanations, learning material, and discussions on its musicianly uses, visit flucoma.org.

method.name: reset
method.digest: Return the object to its original state
method.description: Calling reset will change parameter values back to those the object was created with, i.e. any attribute values set in the box will be retained

description.p: FFT settings consist of three numbers representing the window size, hop size and FFT size in samples:
The hop size and fft size can both be set to -1 (and are by default), with slightly different meanings:

ul.li: For the FFT size, -1 = windowsize snapped to the nearest equal / greater power of 2 (e.g. windowsize 1024 => fftsize 1024, but windowssize 1000 also => fftsize 1024)
FFTSize, if != -1, will snap to the next greatest power of two > 4
The maximum FFT size is limited to the value of the maxfftsize argument
if FFT size != -1, then window size is clipped at FFT size

description.p: The size (in hops) of a smoothing filter that is applied on the novelty curve. A larger filter size allows for cleaner cuts on very sharp changes.

For certain metrics (HFC, SpectralFlux, MKL, Cosine), the distance does not have to be computed between consecutive frames. By default (0) it is, otherwise this sets the distance between the comparison window in samples.
Set an explicit upper bound on the FFT size at object instantiation. The default of -1 sets this to whatever the initial FFT size is
The metric used to derive a difference curve between spectral frames. It can be any of the following:
Energy thresholds on (sum of squares of magnitudes / nBins) (like Onsets power)
HFC thresholds on (sum of (squared magnitudes * binNum) / nBins)
SpectralFlux thresholds on (difference in magnitude between consecutive frames, half rectified)
MKL thresholds on (sum of log of magnitude ratio per bin) (or equivalently, sum of difference of the log magnitude per bin) (like Onsets mkl)
Cosine thresholds on (cosine distance between comparison frames)
PhaseDev takes the past 2 frames, projects to the current, as anticipated if it was a steady state, then compute the sum of the differences, on which it thresholds (like Onsets phase)
WPhaseDev same as PhaseDev, but weighted by the magnitude in order to remove chaos noise floor (like Onsets wphase)

ComplexDev same as PhaseDev, but in the complex domain - the anticipated amp is considered steady, and the phase is projected, then a complex subtraction is done with the actual present frame. The sum of magnitudes is used to threshold (like Onsets complex)
RComplexDev same as above, but rectified (like Onsets rcomplex)
Enable warnings to be issued whenever a parameter value is constrained (e.g. clipped)
